Title: Teosinte Pollen Drive guides maize diversification and domestication by RNAi

Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: W22 reference/background; also surveyed in B73 and broader Zea populations
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: W22-derived TPD lines; B73 for haplotype test
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GDSL triacylglycerol lipase/esterase; catalytic GDSxxDxG motif
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: required for pollen viability and male fertility
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: post-meiotic from 3-mm tetrad stage, peaks in 5-mm anthers and mature pollen; highly pollen specific
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: same in TPD experiments; protein present in wild-type anthers and pollen but absent in TPD
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Tdr1 is required for pollen viability; its silencing or knockout causes developmental arrest and pollen abortion phenocopying Tpd1
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: small RNA target prediction; iPARE-seq; immunoblotting; esterase assay; CRISPR-Cas9 frameshift mutants; pollen viability phenotyping
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tdr1 encodes a pollen-expressed GDSL lipase essential for pollen viability. 2. It is the main responder/target of Tpd1-derived 22-nt siRNAs, which reduce TDR1 protein without lowering mRNA. 3. CRISPR-induced tdr1 mutants phenocopy TPD male sterility, confirming causal function.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: TPD hybrid background; pollen developmental stage
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of Tdr1 causes severe male sterility and delayed anthesis
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Tpd1-derived hp-siRNAs; DCL2-dependent secondary siRNA machinery
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target-site edits or natural immune haplotypes could be used to avoid TPD-mediated sterility during breeding and introgression
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; small RNA-seq; iPARE-seq; western blot; resequencing survey of 1,483 genomes; GEO GSE234925
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Natural in-frame deletion/SNP haplotypes at Tpd1 hp-siRNA target sites in Tdr1 spread in maize and are predicted to confer resistance to silencing.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: suppresses toxic secondary silencing to preserve pollen viability
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mapped to a 1.5-Mb interval on chromosome 6L; contains pollen-expressed candidate genes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as an unlinked gametophytic antidote required for pollen survival; suppresses secondary small RNA biogenesis from Tdr1 and restores fertility in combination with linked dcl2T
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic segregation analysis; bulk segregant analysis; mapping; small RNA profiling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tpd2 is an unlinked antidote locus on chromosome 6 associated with fertility in TPD plants. 2. It is insufficient alone, but together with dcl2T suppresses Tpd1-mediated pollen abortion. 3. Tpd2 acts downstream of the Tpd1 hairpin by suppressing secondary small RNAs from Tdr1.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: male gametophytic transmission in TPD hybrids
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Absent or recombined away from Tpd1 leads to sterility; molecular identity remains unknown
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relevant for managing fertility and introgression barriers, but causal gene is unresolved
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: whole-genome sequencing; bulk segregant analysis; single-pollen sequencing; small RNA-seq; GEO GSE234925; SRA BioProject PRJNA937229
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The molecular identity of Tpd2 is unknown; one candidate in the interval is an Rdm1 homologue, but this is not functionally confirmed and should not be treated as a core causal gene.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Zea mays ssp. mexicana / Zea mays ssp. mays
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: mexicana collection 107 plant 4; W22 background for dcl2-mu1
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Dicer-like RNase III family; DExD/H RNA helicase domain; dsRBD
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Processes hairpin and double-stranded RNA precursors to generate 22-nt siRNAs involved in RNA silencing
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for production of toxic and secondary siRNAs that suppress Tdr1 and determine pollen viability
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: RNA silencing signals that in this system cause pollen abortion by repressing a pollen lipase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Dcl2T maintained in a mexicana introgression interval; Dcl2 expressed pre-meiotically and again in mature pollen
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: pre-meiotic anthers and mature pollen; not in bicellular microspores according to cited single-cell RNA-seq
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wild-type Dcl2 activity promotes Tpd1-mediated pollen abortion through 22-nt siRNA production; the mexicana dcl2T allele acts as a partial antidote and dcl2-mu1 strongly suppresses sterility
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic recombination analysis; mutant analysis with dcl2-mu1; small RNA-seq; pollen viability assays; rescue of seed set
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A mexicana-derived dcl2T allele is tightly pseudolinked with Tpd1 through the male germ line. 2. Dcl2 is required for 22-nt siRNA accumulation in pollen and for the TPD pollen-abortion phenotype. 3. The hypomorphic dcl2-mu1 allele suppresses Tpd1-mediated sterility, while dcl2T behaves as a linked partial antidote.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for 22-nt siRNA production in this drive system
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: male transmission through pollen; maize-teosinte hybrid background
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss or reduction of Dcl2 activity suppresses drive but may alter endogenous RNA silencing functions
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: whole-genome sequencing; single-pollen sequencing; small RNA-seq; RNA-seq; single-cell RNA-seq (published dataset context); GEO GSE234925; SRA BioProject PRJNA937229
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper emphasizes allele-specific behavior: mexicana-derived dcl2T is hypomorphic/partially functional and acts as a linked antidote, whereas Dcl2 activity is also necessary for toxin-associated 22-nt siRNA biogenesis.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: long non-coding hairpin RNA / small-RNA-generating distorter locus
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Functions through AGO-loaded hp-siRNAs and RNAi machinery including DCL2, RGD1/SGS3 and AGO proteins
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: post-transcriptional/translational silencing of a pollen lipid-metabolism gene
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: RNA silencing via 22-nt hp-siRNAs causing cleavage and translational repression in trans
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: represses Tdr1 by hp-siRNA-guided cleavage and translational inhibition, promoting secondary siRNA production from Tdr1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: hairpin expressed pre-meiotically in 1–3 mm anthers and in microspores, not mature pollen
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: presence of the mexicana Tpd1 haplotype
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: promotes secondary siRNA amplification from Tdr1 after initial targeting
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: primary toxin/distorter of the TPD system; necessary and sufficient for dominant male sterility in the absence of antidotes
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Triggers pollen abortion and segregation distortion by silencing Tdr1
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: genetic mapping; bulk segregant analysis; small RNA-seq; iPARE-seq; genome assembly comparison with W22; correlation with pollen abortion phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Tpd1 is a mexicana-derived long non-coding hairpin that produces abundant 22-nt hp-siRNAs specifically in TPD pollen. 2. These siRNAs target Tdr1 in trans and trigger pollen abortion. 3. Tpd1 behaves as the toxin/distorter component of the male-specific Teosinte Pollen Drive system.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for understanding introgression barriers and domestication; likely not desirable to deploy directly because of sterility and segregation distortion
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Causes severe male fertility defects and segregation distortion
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: whole-genome sequencing; de novo genome assembly; small RNA-seq; iPARE-seq; GEO GSE234925; SRA BioProject PRJNA937229
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: W22 carries transposon insertions disrupting this hairpin, making the locus non-functional in maize reference background.
